tenders.title: Short Listing of Consultants (General / Project Consultant) for Implementation of Delhi MRTS Project Phase-IV — Contract DCS-1B
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Consultancy fee to be quoted at RFP stage (EOI/shortlisting stage)
tenders.scopeSummary: Expression of Interest to shortlist National/International consultants to act as General/Project Consultant for implementation of Delhi Metro Phase-IV. The consultant's role is largely advisory across project planning, programming and monitoring, civil engineering, design & structure, construction quality assurance, signalling, telecom, AFC, track, ventilation/AC, BMS, rolling stock and SHE. Shortlisted firms are subsequently issued the RFP for Contract DCS-1B.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: At least one General/Project consultancy work of value >= INR 85.76 Cr on an elevated/underground rail-based Urban MRTS in last 7 years; if no underground component, at least one underground MRTS GC work of >= INR 42.88 Cr. Each JV member must have one similar work >= INR 42.88 Cr.
keyPersonnel: Team Leader (heads the entire consulting team)
Expatriate and local experts across civil, systems, track, rolling stock, SHE
other: Average annual turnover of last 5 financial years > INR 71.47 Cr (consultancy)
Positive net worth in last audited FY
Liquidity / bid capacity criteria
Shortlisting under Guidelines for Employment of Consultants under Japanese ODA Loans, April 2012

technicalCriteria.criterion: Relevant GC/PC experience on elevated/underground rail MRTS
subCriteria: Value and number of similar MRTS consultancy works in last 7 years
technicalCriteria.notes: Eligibility gate at EOI; scored in the RFP technical proposal

technicalCriteria.criterion: Financial standing (turnover, net worth, liquidity, bid capacity)

technicalCriteria.notes: Detailed technical scoring applied to shortlisted firms at RFP stage under QCBS 80:20

tenders.sourceDocument: DMRC EOI for Short Listing of Consultants — Contract DCS-1B, Delhi MRTS Phase-IV

tenders.title: General Consultancy Services for Lucknow MRTS Project — Phase 1B (LKGC (02)-01)
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Consultancy fee to be quoted; EMD Rs 5.25 Cr
tenders.scopeSummary: General Consultancy for the Lucknow Metro Phase 1B (East-West Corridor). Scope includes independent quality monitoring, safety supervision, interface reviews, project implementation planning and reporting, including preparation of a Quality and Safety Monitoring Plan (QSMP) covering inspections, audits and verification of contractors' quality-assurance and safety compliance. Completion period 50 months from Notice To Proceed.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: General/Project consultancy experience on rail-based Urban Mass Rapid Transit systems (per RFP data sheet)
other: EMD Rs 5.25 Cr
Pre-bid meeting 19 Jun 2026; bid submission end 09 Jul 2026
Bid via CPP / UPMRC e-procurement

tenders.sourceDocument: UPMRC NIT LKGC (02)-01 — General Consultancy for Lucknow Metro Phase 1B (2026)

tenders.title: Project Management Consultancy (PMC) Services for Construction of Civil Works Packages of the Mumbai–Ahmedabad High Speed Rail (MAHSR) Corridor
tenders.issuingAuthority: National High Speed Rail Corporation Ltd (NHSRCL)
tenders.scopeSummary: Project Management Consultancy for the construction of civil works packages of the 508.17 km Mumbai–Ahmedabad High Speed Rail corridor, over a 96-month tenure. The PMC works alongside the Japanese-led General Consultant to manage construction execution. Selection was by QCBS; the winning JV (Tata Consulting Engineers–Consulting Engineers Group–Aarvee Associates–PADECO) quoted ₹1,111 Cr.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Experience in project management / construction supervision of high-speed rail or comparable large rail/infrastructure projects (per RFP)
other: International Competitive Bidding; JICA-assisted
Consortium/JV permitted

technicalCriteria.criterion: Firm's relevant HSR / large rail infrastructure PMC experience
technicalCriteria.notes: Qualified bidders' final technical scores were 99.25 (TCE JV) and 97.25 (Nippon Koei–OCG–RITES JV) out of 100

tenders.sourceDocument: NHSRCL RFP for PMC — MAHSR civil works (Notice NHSRCL/CO/CA/MA-SVGC/2020/34)

tenders.title: Appointment of General Consultant for Ghat Section of Taranga Hill–Ambaji–Aburoad New Railway Line Project (including Tunnels), North Western Railway
tenders.issuingAuthority: Indian Port Rail & Ropeway Corporation Ltd (IPRCL) for North Western Railway (Construction HQ)
tenders.scopeSummary: General Consultancy for construction of the ghat (hilly) section of the Taranga Hill–Ambaji–Aburoad new railway line, including tunnels, tall bridges, deep cuttings and slope stabilisation, on behalf of North Western Railway. The GC reviews designs, supervises construction and monitors physical/financial progress. Selection is single-stage two-packet under QCBS. This RFP carries a detailed marks-based technical scoring table used in the framework.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Eligible Assignments (railway/tunnel/infrastructure consultancy) completed in last 5 financial years; each qualifying assignment credited 10 marks up to 200
other: Team Leader must be on bidder's payroll >=15 days before Bid Due Date
Same CV proposed by two bidders = zero marks for both
Up to 25% of Key Personnel may fail eligibility (scored zero) without disqualifying the bid
Skill tests / technical presentation for key personnel

subCriteria: 10 marks per completed Eligible Assignment in last 5 FY (max 200)
technicalCriteria.notes: Raw marks folded into the 70-point technical scale; min qualifying technical score 49/70 (70%)

technicalCriteria.criterion: Average annual revenue from professional/consultancy fees (last 3 FY)
subCriteria: 1 mark per crore of average annual professional fees (max 130)
5 marks per Eligible Assignment as Team Leader (max 50)
4 marks per assignment / per year for retired Railway/PSU staff (max 20)

technicalCriteria.criterion: Proposed methodology & work plan + technical presentation and skill tests
subCriteria: Skill tests / Q&A / written tests of key personnel
technicalCriteria.notes: Methodology & work plan carries 60 marks weight; skill-test performance affects key-personnel marks

tenders.sourceDocument: IPRCL RFP — General Consultant, Taranga Hill–Ambaji–Aburoad ghat section incl. tunnels, NWR (2025)

tenders.title: Selection of Project Management Company (PMC) for PMC Services and Detailed Design Consultant (DDC) for Chennai Metro Rail Phase 1 Network Capacity Enhancement Project
tenders.scopeSummary: Combined engagement of a Project Management Company for PMC services and a Detailed Design Consultant for the Chennai Metro Rail Phase 1 Network Capacity Enhancement Project. Scope covers project management consultancy plus detailed design of civil, systems and station works (Section 5A Detailed Design Consultant scope). National Competitive Bidding under a single-stage two-envelope (technical & financial) QCBS system.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Experience in PMC / detailed design of metro or comparable urban rail systems (per qualification criteria in tender)
other: Single-stage two-envelope (technical & financial)
MSE/Udyam preferences applicable
Bid via CPP Portal

tenders.sourceDocument: CMRL NIT — PMC & DDC for Chennai Metro Phase 1 Network Capacity Enhancement (2025)

tenders.title: Detailed Design Consultant for Property Development above Entry/Exit of Underground Metro Stations in Corridor-3, CMRL Phase-2
tenders.scopeSummary: Detailed Design Consultant for property development above the entry/exit structures of underground metro stations at Madhavaram High Road, Sembiyum, Ayanavaram, Otteri, Perambur Barracks, Purasaiwalkam and Thirumayilai in Corridor-3 of CMRL Phase-2. Contract duration 730 days; single-stage two-envelope QCBS.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Detailed design experience for metro/transit property development or comparable RCC/steel-framed built structures (per qualification criteria)
other: Single-stage two-envelope (technical & financial) under QCBS
Bid via CPP Portal

tenders.sourceDocument: CMRL NIT CMRL/CP/TDR-56-CS-DDC/2025 — DDC for property development over UG station entry/exits, Corridor-3

tenders.title: Project Management Consultancy Services for Redevelopment of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus (CSMT) Station and Associated Infrastructure
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Consultancy fee to be quoted; EMD INR 25 Lakh
tenders.scopeSummary: Appointment of an agency to provide Project Management Consultancy for the redevelopment of CSMT station and construction of associated infrastructure. Period of services 30 months. Bidders demonstrate organisational technical capacity via similar passenger-terminal / large real-estate consultancy assignments.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: In last 7 years (ending 31.12.2022): three similar works with technical consulting fee >= INR 10 Cr each, OR two >= INR 20 Cr each, OR one >= INR 30 Cr. 'Similar' = passenger terminals (railway/metro/RRTS/HSR/monorail stations, airport/bus terminals) or large RCC/steel commercial-institutional real estate.
other: EMD INR 25 Lakh
Minimum one ongoing technical consultancy assignment >= INR 50 Cr secured in last 5 years
Lead JV member must meet the two-project (or one large-project) experience test

subCriteria: Value & number of eligible assignments in last 7 years

technicalCriteria.criterion: Ongoing large consultancy assignment (>= INR 50 Cr)

tenders.sourceDocument: RLDA RFP CT-04 of 2023 — PMC for CSMT station redevelopment

tenders.title: Project Management Consultancy Services for Redevelopment of Ahmedabad Railway Station and Associated Infrastructure
tenders.scopeSummary: Appointment of an agency to provide Project Management Consultancy for the redevelopment of Ahmedabad Railway Station and associated infrastructure. Eligibility mirrors RLDA's standard station-redevelopment PMC template with higher fee thresholds than CSMT.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: In last 7 years (ending 31.12.2022): three similar works with technical consulting fee >= INR 15 Cr each, OR two >= INR 25 Cr each, OR one >= INR 30 Cr. 'Similar' = passenger terminals (railway/metro/RRTS/HSR/monorail, airport/bus) or large RCC/steel commercial-institutional real estate.
other: Minimum one ongoing technical consultancy assignment >= INR 50 Cr secured in last 5 years
Lead member must meet two-project (or one large-project) experience test

technicalCriteria.criterion: Ongoing large consultancy assignment (>= INR 50 Cr)

tenders.sourceDocument: RLDA RFP — PMC for Ahmedabad station redevelopment (2023)

tenders.title: Consultancy for Techno-Economic Feasibility Study, Master Planning, Urban Design, Engineering & DPR for Integrated Redevelopment of Delhi Sarai Rohilla Railway Station
tenders.scopeSummary: Consultancy for techno-economic feasibility study, detailed master planning, urban design, engineering and preparation of the DPR for integrated redevelopment of Delhi Sarai Rohilla station (Northern Railway). Limited tender inviting only RLDA-empanelled architectural firms (Category A, station categories NSG-01/02 and important stations). Selection is QCBS among empanelled firms via single-stage two-packet; there is no separate eligibility test (empanelment substitutes for it).

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Restricted to firms empanelled under RLDA Category A (architectural consultancy for NSG-01/02 & important stations); empanelment already establishes prior experience
other: Limited (empanelled) tender
Single-stage two-packet
Master plan must support modular/phased construction; feasibility & preliminary design feed the downstream developer RFP

technicalCriteria.criterion: Quality of master plan / feasibility approach (only evaluation criteria; no eligibility)
technicalCriteria.notes: Tenders from empanelled consultants specify evaluation criteria only

tenders.sourceDocument: RLDA RFP CT-51 of 2022 — Feasibility/master plan/DPR for Delhi Sarai Rohilla station redevelopment

tenders.title: Final Location Survey (FLS), Geological/Traffic/Techno-Economic Studies, Alignment Design & DPR for Reasi–Poonch New BG Railway Line, J&K
tenders.issuingAuthority: Ministry of Railways — Northern Railway (CAO/USBRL, Jammu)
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Survey/DPR consultancy fee to be quoted; EMD INR 14,03,800
tenders.scopeSummary: Final Location Survey with associated geological studies, traffic survey, techno-economic studies, design of railway alignment and preparation of the Detailed Project Report for the Reasi–Poonch new broad-gauge railway line in Jammu & Kashmir. Tendered through IREPS/CPPP by Northern Railway (USBRL). A representative example of the railway FLS/DPR survey-consultancy category.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Experience in railway FLS / alignment design / DPR preparation as per Railway Board latest survey guidelines (per tender document)
other: EMD INR 14,03,800
Bid via IREPS / CPPP
Deliverables: DPR, L-section, ESP, GAD of bridges, land plans, hydrological & traffic surveys, geotechnical investigation, estimate

technicalCriteria.notes: Detailed scoring per the tender document; MoR survey consultancies commonly use two-packet QCBS

tenders.sourceDocument: MoR/Northern Railway (USBRL) tender 75W126WA-FLS-Reasi-Poonch — FLS + DPR, Reasi–Poonch new line

tenders.title: Final Location Survey (FLS) incl. DPR for New BG Railway Lines — Gazol–Itahar, Itahar–Raiganj, Itahar–Buniadpur & Raiganj–Dalkhola, N.F. Railway
tenders.issuingAuthority: Ministry of Railways — N.F. Railway (Construction), HQ Maligaon, Guwahati
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Survey/DPR consultancy fee to be quoted; EMD INR 8,10,500
tenders.scopeSummary: Final Location Survey for four new broad-gauge line sections — Gazol–Itahar (~27.2 km), Itahar–Raiganj (~21.825 km), Itahar–Buniadpur (~39 km) and Raiganj–Dalkhola (~43.43 km) — including submission of the DPR, L-section, ESP, GAD of all bridges, land plans, hydrological survey, traffic survey, geotechnical investigation and revised estimate as per latest Railway Board guidelines. Tendered by N.F. Railway (Construction) via IREPS.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Railway FLS / DPR / alignment survey experience per Railway Board guidelines (per tender document)
other: EMD INR 8,10,500
Bid via IREPS / CPPP
Multiple contiguous sections in a single package

tenders.sourceDocument: MoR/N.F. Railway tender CE-CON-NL-FLS-2026-01 — FLS + DPR for four new BG line sections

tenders.title: Selection of Consultants (DPR Consultant, General Engineering Consultant & Detailed Design Consultant) for Surat Metro Rail Project
tenders.issuingAuthority: Gujarat Metro Rail Corporation (GMRC) — Surat Metro
tenders.scopeSummary: Consultant-selection strategy and criteria for the Surat Metro Rail Project covering the DPR Consultant (prepares DPR & preliminary designs), General Engineering Consultant (construction supervision) and Detailed Design Consultant (detailed civil/structural/architectural, building services and E&M design of viaduct and elevated/underground stations). Selection under QCBS with a minimum technical score of 70. Illustrates typical metro DDC/GEC eligibility and evaluation structure.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: For DDC: detailed design of viaduct and elevated/underground stations for Metro/HSR/Light Rail/Railways valued at >= 80% of total tender cost; Team Leader with >=15 years and >=2 projects of similar nature; each expert >=15 years and >=3 similar projects
keyPersonnel: Team Leader / Project Leader (>=15 yrs, 2 similar projects)
other: Average annual turnover from mass-transport consultancy (last 3 FY, from 2013-14) >= INR 20 Cr
Minimum technical score 70 to qualify
Financial score: lowest bid = 100 marks (inverse proportional)

technicalCriteria.criterion: Firm's experience in similar mass-transit projects (last 7 years)

tenders.sourceDocument: Surat Metro (GMRC) — Selection of Consultants (DPR/GEC/DDC) strategy & criteria document

tenders.title: Manual for Procurement of Consultancy & Other Services (MoF, Department of Expenditure, updated June 2022)
tenders.issuingAuthority: Ministry of Finance, Department of Expenditure (Government of India)
tenders.scopeSummary: The umbrella Government of India manual (under GFR 2017, Rule 192) that defines the consultant-selection systems all railway/metro authorities implement: QCBS, Least Cost Selection (LCS), Quality-Based Selection (QBS/FBS), Fixed Budget and Single Source. It sets the rule that the weightage of technical (non-financial) parameters must not exceed 80%, and requires the RFP to specify the minimum qualifying technical score and the technical:financial weightage.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Defined per assignment in each RFP
other: QCBS technical weight capped at 80% (Rule 192, GFR 2017)
RFP must state minimum qualifying technical score and quality:cost weightage
Applicable across central PSUs incl. RVNL/RITES/DFCCIL/NHSRCL and metro corporations

technicalCriteria.criterion: Specific experience relevant to the assignment

tenders.sourceDocument: Manual for Procurement of Consultancy & Other Services, MoF (June 2022)

evaluationFramework.summary:
Consultant selection across the Indian railway and metro sector is dominated by Quality & Cost Based Selection (QCBS), applied under GFR 2017 Rule 192 and the MoF Manual for Procurement of Consultancy & Other Services (2022). Metro corporations (DMRC, UPMRC, CMRL, BMRCL, MahaMetro, GMRC) and JICA-assisted projects (DMRC Phase-IV DCS-1B, NHSRCL MAHSR) almost always use an 80:20 technical:financial split, while railway zonal/PSU general-consultancy RFPs (e.g. IPRCL/NWR) sometimes use a 70:30 structure (technical scored out of 70). Large metro GC/PMC contracts frequently begin with an Expression of Interest / shortlisting stage that imposes hard eligibility gates (a single similar MRTS consultancy work worth tens of crore, average annual turnover in the tens of crore, positive net worth) before the QCBS-scored RFP is issued to shortlisted firms.

The minimum qualifying technical score is typically 70 out of 100 (metro DDC/GEC/PMC and GMRC Surat), or 70% (49/70 in the IPRCL railway GC RFP); NHSRCL's MAHSR PMC saw qualified bidders score 97-99 out of 100, showing how competitive the top of the market is. Technical marks are dominated by two blocks: the firm's relevant experience (number and value of similar rail/metro/station consultancy assignments in the last 5-7 years) and the qualifications of key personnel (Team Leader plus discipline experts), with average annual consultancy revenue/turnover scored as a supporting sub-criterion (e.g. 1 mark per crore up to a cap in the IPRCL RFP) and approach/methodology plus a technical presentation/skill test carrying a smaller but decisive weight.

Eligibility thresholds are strongly value-based and vary by asset type: metro GC (DMRC Phase-IV) needs a similar work of ~₹85.76 Cr and turnover > ₹71.47 Cr; station-redevelopment PMC (RLDA CSMT/Ahmedabad) needs 1-3 similar consultancy works of ₹10-30 Cr plus an ongoing assignment > ₹50 Cr; metro DDC/GEC (Surat) needs ₹20 Cr average annual consultancy turnover and design experience worth ~80% of the tender cost. Common cross-cutting rules include: the same CV cannot be proposed by two bidders (zero marks to both), the Team Leader must be on the bidder's payroll, Council of Architecture registration is mandatory for station-redevelopment architectural work, and JV/consortium credit is apportioned by participation share.

criteria.label: Firm's Relevant Rail/Metro Consultancy Experience
criteria.description: Track record of similar consultancy assignments matched to the tender type: general/project consultancy on rail-based MRTS (metro GC), PMC on HSR/large rail works, detailed design of viaducts and stations (DDC), FLS/DPR of new railway lines, or station-redevelopment/master-planning. Credited by number and value of qualifying assignments completed in the last 5-7 years (e.g. IPRCL: 10 marks per Eligible Assignment up to 200) and apportioned by JV participation share.

scoringGuidance.meaning: Multiple (3+) high-value similar assignments, including a flagship work above the tender's single-work value gate (e.g. > ₹85 Cr metro GC / > ₹30 Cr station PMC), with directly comparable scope
2 qualifying similar assignments comfortably above the value floor; solid recent metro/rail track record
Meets the minimum floor: one similar assignment at the largest single-work threshold, or three at the smaller threshold
Below the eligibility value/number floor, or experience mainly as a minor JV partner / sub-consultant (not counted)

inputFields.label: Number of similar rail/metro consultancy assignments completed in last 7 years (matching this tender type: GC/PMC/DDC/DPR-FLS/station)
inputFields.scoring: 1 = Fair (floor); 2 = Good; 3+ = Excellent (IPRCL credits 10 marks each up to 200)

inputFields.label: Value of your single largest qualifying similar assignment (₹)
inputFields.scoring: Compared against the tender's single-work gate: metro GC ~₹85.76 Cr; station PMC ₹30 Cr; underground MRTS GC ₹42.88 Cr; DDC ~80% of tender cost

inputFields.label: Closest asset type of your cited experience
inputFields.scoring: Underground/HSR/tunnel experience scores higher for matching tenders; unrelated infrastructure is discounted

inputFields.label: Typical role of the firm in cited assignments
inputFields.scoring: Credit apportioned by JV participation share; sub-consultancy generally excluded

criteria.label: Average Annual Turnover / Consultancy Revenue
criteria.description: Average annual turnover (or professional/consultancy fee revenue), certified by a Chartered Accountant, over the last 3-5 financial years. Acts as an eligibility gate (e.g. DMRC Phase-IV > ₹71.47 Cr; Surat metro ₹20 Cr) and, in railway GC RFPs, as a directly scored sub-criterion (IPRCL: 1 mark per crore of average annual professional fees up to 130). Positive net worth and liquidity/bid-capacity are usually also required.

scoringGuidance.meaning: Average annual consultancy turnover comfortably exceeds the tender gate (e.g. > ₹70-130 Cr for large metro GC/GC-scored RFPs)
Turnover well above the floor (e.g. ₹30-70 Cr) with positive net worth
Turnover just clears the tender gate (e.g. ₹20 Cr metro DDC/GEC)
Turnover below the specified minimum, negative net worth, or insufficient bid capacity (ineligible)

inputFields.label: Average annual turnover from consultancy business (last 3-5 years, ₹)
inputFields.scoring: Must clear the tender gate (₹20 Cr Surat DDC/GEC up to ₹71.47 Cr DMRC Phase-IV GC); scored 1 mark/crore up to a cap in railway GC RFPs

inputFields.label: Is your net worth positive as per the last audited financial year?
inputFields.scoring: Positive net worth is a mandatory financial-standing gate

criteria.label: Qualifications & Competence of Key Personnel
criteria.description: The other dominant block. Each key expert (Team Leader plus discipline experts — for railway/metro: tunnel/bridge design, geology, track, systems, station architecture, finance) is scored on qualification, total and similar-capacity experience, and minimum days of deployment. The Team Leader must be on the bidder's payroll; the same CV proposed by two bidders scores zero for both; some RFPs require a technical presentation and skill test of key personnel.

scoringGuidance.meaning: All key experts exceed the essential qualification with extensive similar-capacity experience on comparable metro/rail projects; Team Leader well above 15 years and multiple similar assignments
Full team meets requirements; Team Leader has 15+ years and 2+ similar assignments; most experts strong on similar experience
Team meets minimum qualifications; some experts light on directly-similar experience
One or more key positions under-qualified or unfilled; > 25% of key personnel failing eligibility risks disqualification

inputFields.label: Team Leader — total professional experience (years)
inputFields.scoring: Typically >=15 years required; more years and seniority score higher

inputFields.label: Team Leader — number of similar assignments in a lead/senior role
inputFields.scoring: IPRCL: 5 marks per eligible assignment as TL (up to 50); Surat requires 2 similar projects

inputFields.label: Is the proposed Team Leader on your firm's payroll (before bid due date)?
inputFields.scoring: Mandatory in railway GC RFPs; if No, marks/eligibility affected

inputFields.label: Number of required key-expert positions you can fill with qualified, exclusive CVs
inputFields.scoring: Metro GC/PMC needs a broad multidisciplinary team; > 25% of positions failing eligibility can disqualify the bid

inputFields.label: Is any proposed CV also being submitted by another bidder for this tender?
inputFields.scoring: If Yes, that CV scores zero for all bidders proposing it

criteria.label: Approach, Methodology, Work Plan & Presentation
criteria.description: Quality of the technical approach, methodology and work plan, plus (in several railway/metro RFPs) a technical presentation and skill test of the Team Leader and key personnel. Includes project understanding, staffing/organisation, and for metro GC a Quality & Safety Monitoring Plan (QSMP).

scoringGuidance.meaning: Sharp project appreciation, robust and realistic methodology, clear work plan/organisation, strong presentation and skill-test performance
Sound methodology and work plan with minor gaps; competent presentation
Generic methodology largely restating the ToR
Weak/boilerplate approach; poor understanding or presentation

inputFields.label: Self-assessed quality of your approach & methodology (incl. QSMP for metro GC)
inputFields.scoring: Maps to scoring guidance; e.g. 60 marks weight for methodology in the IPRCL GC RFP

inputFields.label: Can your Team Leader and key experts attend a technical presentation / skill test?
inputFields.scoring: Several railway/metro RFPs require a presentation + Q&A / written skill test that directly affects marks

criteria.label: Statutory & Eligibility Compliance
criteria.description: Pass/fail compliance gates specific to railway/metro consultancy: Council of Architecture registration (station-redevelopment architectural work), RLDA empanelment for limited station tenders, EMD/tender-fee payment, bid capacity, JV/consortium structuring rules, and no debarment/vigilance history for proposed personnel.

scoringGuidance.meaning: All statutory registrations valid, EMD paid, bid capacity met, clean personnel records, JV structured per rules
Compliant with minor documentation to update before submission
Some registrations/empanelment pending (must be fixed before submission)
Missing mandatory registration/empanelment, unpaid EMD, or personnel with adverse vigilance/CBI records (bid rejected)

inputFields.label: For station-redevelopment/architectural work: is the firm/architect registered with the Council of Architecture?
inputFields.scoring: Mandatory for RLDA architectural consultancy; non-registration = non-responsive

inputFields.label: For limited RLDA station tenders: is the firm empanelled in the required category?
inputFields.scoring: Limited tenders are open only to empanelled firms

inputFields.label: Are all proposed key personnel free of major-penalty vigilance/CBI/police records?
inputFields.scoring: Adverse records disqualify the CV / affect eligibility

keyPolicies.name: General Financial Rules 2017, Rule 192 — Quality and Cost Based Selection (QCBS)
keyPolicies.relevance: Statutory basis for QCBS; caps technical (non-financial) weight at 80% and requires the RFP to specify the minimum qualifying technical score and quality:cost weightage.

keyPolicies.name: Manual for Procurement of Consultancy & Other Services (MoF, Dept. of Expenditure, June 2022)
keyPolicies.relevance: Umbrella policy defining consultant-selection systems (QCBS, LCS, QBS/FBS, Fixed Budget, SSS) implemented by railway/metro authorities.

keyPolicies.name: Guidelines for the Employment of Consultants under Japanese ODA Loans (April 2012)
keyPolicies.relevance: Governs shortlisting and QCBS 80:20 selection for JICA-assisted metro/HSR consultancy (DMRC Phase-IV DCS-1B, NHSRCL MAHSR).
